prompt_text:
You are shown a SKELETON view of a java repository: class headers,
inheritance, method signatures, and attributes — but NO method bodies.

Use this signature-level view to detect structural violations of Dependency Inversion Principle (DIP):
- Fat interfaces / bloated classes (many unrelated method groups)
- Clients forced to implement methods they don't need (abstract methods / NotImplementedError / empty overrides)
- Inheritance hierarchies where subclasses alter the contract shape
- Broad API...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/ackedqueue/Queue.java",
      "entity_name": "Queue.Queue",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 102,
      "line_end": 107,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Hard-coded instantiation of dependencies via constructor",
      "reasoning": "The Queue class is responsible for creating its own dependencies (Settings, Metric) via hard-coded constructor logic. It should instead receive these dependencies through constructor injection to allow for easier testing and dependency management."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/execution/AbstractPipelineExt.java",
      "entity_name": "AbstractPipelineExt.createDeadLetterQueueWriterFromSettings",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 398,
      "line_end": 398,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "High-level business logic instantiating low-level implementation detail",
      "reasoning": "The AbstractPipelineExt class is creating a DeadLetterQueueWriter instance directly. High-level modules should rely on abstractions, not concrete implementations of writers."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/config/ir/CompiledPipeline.java",
      "entity_name": "CompiledPipeline.CompiledPipeline",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 113,
      "line_end": 120,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Direct dependency on concrete PluginFactory implementation",
      "reasoning": "The constructor forces a dependency on RubyIntegration.PluginFactory. While an interface, it is highly coupled to Ruby-specific integration logic within a Java compiled pipeline, violating the separation of concerns by leaking infrastructure details into the business logic."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/plugins/factory/PluginFactoryExt.java",
      "entity_name": "PluginFactoryExt.buildInput",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 119,
      "line_end": 122,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Factory method directly instantiating plugins without abstraction injection",
      "reasoning": "The method is responsible for instantiating plugins directly. In a DIP-compliant architecture, the factory should utilize a registry or provider pattern where the injection of the plugin implementation is managed outside of the instantiation logic."
    }
  ]
}

findings.description: Hard-coded instantiation of dependencies via constructor
findings.reasoning: The Queue class is responsible for creating its own dependencies (Settings, Metric) via hard-coded constructor logic. It should instead receive these dependencies through constructor injection to allow for easier testing and dependency management.

findings.description: High-level business logic instantiating low-level implementation detail
findings.reasoning: The AbstractPipelineExt class is creating a DeadLetterQueueWriter instance directly. High-level modules should rely on abstractions, not concrete implementations of writers.

findings.description: Direct dependency on concrete PluginFactory implementation
findings.reasoning: The constructor forces a dependency on RubyIntegration.PluginFactory. While an interface, it is highly coupled to Ruby-specific integration logic within a Java compiled pipeline, violating the separation of concerns by leaking infrastructure details into the business logic.

findings.description: Factory method directly instantiating plugins without abstraction injection
findings.reasoning: The method is responsible for instantiating plugins directly. In a DIP-compliant architecture, the factory should utilize a registry or provider pattern where the injection of the plugin implementation is managed outside of the instantiation logic.
